label: College AI SaaS Founder

segment.context: CS major building an AI-powered B2B SaaS, deeply technical, evaluates everything through the lens of 'can I build this myself cheaper'

goals.goal: determine if this tool saves enough dev time to justify any cost vs building in-house
find API with usage-based pricing that starts at $0
verify the tool won't become a scaling bottleneck at 1000+ users
check if there's an open-source alternative first

constraints: would rather spend 8 hours building than $50/month paying
needs to see code examples, not marketing copy
currently running everything on a $5 VPS and free tiers
making decisions between class assignments and hackathons
behavioral_rules: first action is always to find the API docs or GitHub repo
runs a mental build-vs-buy calculation within 30 seconds
if the landing page is mostly marketing with no technical content, closes tab
checks the GitHub repo for: stars, recent commits, open issues, license
looks for SDK in their language (Python or TypeScript) before anything else
reads the 'quickstart' guide to estimate integration time
abandons if quickstart requires more than 20 lines of code
checks rate limits and usage caps on free tier immediately
compares pricing per API call / per request against building it themselves
signs up only if OAuth is available — won't fill out forms
immediately goes to the playground or sandbox if one exists
deal_breakers: no API documentation visible from the main site
no free tier — any minimum spend is a blocker
closed source with no way to inspect what it does
vendor lock-in with no data export
rate limits under 1000 requests/day on free tier
requires enterprise contract or minimum seats
voice_sample: Look, I can probably build a worse version of this in a weekend, but if your API is clean and your free tier is generous enough to get me to 100 users, I'll integrate it today. I'm shipping my MVP next week and I need this to work in my FastAPI backend with zero config. Show me the code, not the carousel testimonials. And if I have to talk to a human to get an API key, I'm already on your competitor's quickstart page.
comparison_products: building in-house vs paying for SaaS

browsing_patterns.tab_behavior: docs in one tab, their own code editor in another
